channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: In South Korea, AI chip workers become top marriage prospects
item.description: In South Korea, the AI frenzy has sent the value of the country's top chipmakers skyrocketing. Korea's Samsung Electronics and its chief rival SK Hynix have been trading places as the world's most valuable producer of memory chips. Following some strident union organizing at the firms, their workers have become some of the country's highest-paid - with some performance bonuses at Samsung's chip unit set to top 400,000 dollars this year. That has not only made jobs at the companies highly sought-after: it's also made their employees highly sought-after as marriage prospects.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French Resistant fighter historian Marc Bloch join the Pantheon greats
item.description: He was a leading historian of the 20th-century, and co-founded French academic journal 'Les Annales' with Lucien Febvre, and was also a soldier in both World Wars. Historian Marc Bloch was tortured and executed by the Gestapo in 1944, and will enter the Pantheon on June 23 in a rare tribute reserved for France's most revered historical figures.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'No one in the UK seems happy over Brexit': Reporter Clovis Casali
item.description: As the UK marks a decade since the Brexit vote that took it out of the European Union, our reporter who's been back to gauge the mood in the UK says no one seems happy with the result. To mark the anniversary, FRANCE 24 filmed a special edition of our Revisited programme, talking to some of those most affected by the decision. Clovis Casali says he found that those who voted to remain are still unhappy that the UK is now no longer an EU member, while those who voted to leave say politicians let them down after the referendum and the hard Brexit they wanted never happened. He spoke to us in Perspective.
item.dc:creator: Stuart NORVAL

item.title: In Mexico, the World Cup comes at a price for fans
item.description: The passion for football in Mexico is high,.but so too are the prices. A World Cup ticket can cost hundreds of euros, while a ticket for a match in Mexico normally costs around 40 euros. The price of food has also increased. For the majority of Mexicans, these high prices make the World Cup unaffordable, sometimes even overshadowing the excitement of the tournament.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ten years on, 'jury still out' on Brexit although early signs of damage show
item.description: Ten years ago, the UK voted to leave the European Union with promises that Brexit would help the country economically. But a recent study suggests the economy actually took a six-percent hit. Support for Brexit has decreased: younger voters, who would have not been able to vote at the time, today mostly support a return to the EU if given the chance. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Ten years on, the UK's workers take stock of Brexit
item.description: Ten years on from the Brexit referendum, how has the UK's departure from the European Union impacted the country and its economy? A BBC report said the UK economy had taken a six-percent hit from Brexit, according to analysis of internal Bank of England data. Raw GDP numbers published by the OECD suggest that the UK performed broadly in line with other advanced economies in the years immediately following the 2016 referendum, but started lagging behind the US and Canada since the early 2020s. FRANCE 24's Clovis Casali went to ask British workers.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: France win after weather delay as Messi smashes record
item.description: The Bleus' game against Iraq was interrupted for over two hours due to heavy rain in Philadelphia - the first delay in the World Cup so far caused by the weather. Their 3-0 win sees them book their spot in the knockout round with a game to spare, while Lionel Messi has broken the record for the most goals scored at the World Cup, with a total of 18 goals since 2006.
item.dc:creator: James VASINA

item.title: France issues red alert as heatwave breaks records and death toll mounts
item.description: France experienced its hottest-ever night Monday into Tuesday as Prime Minister Sébastien Lecornu said 40 people had drowned across the country since June 18 as they sought to escape the record heat. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, the scorching temperatures have led to school closures, cancelled events and disrupted rail services.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: 'Temu version': World's largest Messi statue mocked online
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Tuesday, June 23: This Tuesday marks 10 years since millions of British people voted for Brexit. The milestone comes one day after Prime Minister Keir Starmer's tearful resignation. Also, the internet goes crazy for "hot podium guy", a handsome sound engineer who sets up the lectern in front of 10 Downing Street for each big announcement. Indian leader Narendra Modi inaugurates three new Indian-made naval vessels in a move seeking to counter China's growing influence. Finally, a new statue in honour of Lionel Messi is ridiculed as a "Temu" of Messi statues!
item.dc:creator: Dheepthika LAURENT

item.title: French celebrate win against Iraq despite two-hour delay caused by storm
item.description: The weather in the US tried to put a stop to the France-Iraq game at the World Cup on June 22... and it did: the game had to be halted for two hours as a huge thunderstorm hit in Philadelphia, where it was being held. France ended up winning 3-0, with two of them coming from French superstar Kylian Mbappé. But nothing could deter French fans from celebrating.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Tartan Army turns Little Havana into ‘Little Scotland’
item.description: Thousands of Scotland supporters turned Miami’s Little Havana neighbourhood into “Little Scotland” on Monday, with the Tartan Army marching through the streets with bagpipes and drums ahead of their match with Brazil in the city later this week.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Europe faces warmest year on record as heatwave batters continent
item.description: Europeans face sweltering temperatures once again, as another heatwave brings disruption in schools, rail services and sport events, as officials fear heat-related health risks. Temperatures are expected to hit 39 degrees celsius, heading to be the hottest year on record. According to the WHO, over 200 000 people have died from heat-related causes in Europe in the past four years.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: How Andy Burnham can become the next UK prime minister
item.description: Now that Keir Starmer has said he's stepping down as Prime Minister, there's only one name that's circulating to take his place. But Andy Burnham still has to be officially anointed in a vote by Labour party members. Starmer has said that he wants the new leader in place by 1st of September, when parliament returns from recess.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Serious failings found in handling of earlier complaint against main suspect in girl's killing
item.description: Three weeks after the body of 11-year-old Lyhanna was found following her disappearance from the French town of Fleurance, investigators have uncovered serious failings in the handling of an earlier complaint against the main suspect. The Interior Minister has sought disciplinary action against two gendarmerie officers, while the Prime Minister said the inspection revealed that "the chain of protection failed."
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Flamingo Revolution': Albanian protests swell against Trump-linked resort
item.description: Albanians have been gathering in the streets of Tijana ever day for over three weeks. The so-called 'flamingo revolution' started in reaction to the construction of luxury resorts linked to the Trump family in the south of the country on an island where the protected birds nest. France 24 journalist David Gilberg followed a Franco-Albanian woman named Iris as she protested in her home country this weekend. FRANCE 24 went on the ground and spoke to some of them.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Resistance fighter Marc Bloch becomes first historian to enter France's Panthéon
item.description: This Tuesday June 23, Marc Bloch will be inducted into Paris's Panthéon mausoleum. A leading 20th-century historian, he co-founded the French academic journal "Les Annales" and was also a soldier in both World Wars. Bloch, who was Jewish, joined the French Resistance during World War II and was tortured and executed by the Gestapo in 1944. Throughout his life, he embodied both intellectual rigour and an unwavering commitment to the French Republic. FRANCE 24's Florence Gaillard reports, with Florent Marchais.
item.dc:creator: Florence GAILLARD

item.title: Protests erupt in Colombia as far-right set to win presidential election
item.description: Protests have erupted in Colombia against the election of the far-right candidate Abelardo de la Espriella. Though election officials have yet to formally announce a winner, with almost all of the votes counted, the pro-Trump lawyer and businessman is set to defeat his left-wing opponent Ivan Cepeda. Trailing behind by around 250 000 votes, the left-wing senator told supporters his team would be challenging results from tens of thousands of voting stations.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Iran set to return to mainstream oil market as US temporarily waives sanctions
item.description: As 60 days of peace negotiations between the US and Iran continue, Tehran appears to have won significant concessions from Washington. Chief among those is the waiving of sanctions on Iranian oil exports. US sanctions have been in place for decades, cutting Iran off from mainstream global oil markets and forcing it to sell its oil to China at discount rates. The US says the other side of the deal is that Iran will allow international inspectors access to its nuclear program - Tehran though says it has made no such commitment.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Messi becomes World Cup all-time goal scorer in game against Austria
item.description: France's second World Cup game belonged to Mbappé as he scored twice, moving into a tie for second in World Cup goals. Argentina won its second game against Austria, with two goals scored by Messi. Norway's Haaland also scored two goals in the game against Senegal, whose fate now depends on its game against Iraq. FRANCE 24's James Vasina tells us what to know.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France faces historically hot temperatures as heatwave carries on
item.description: Millions of people across France woke up drenched in sweat on June 23 after another night of scorching heat, with most of the population exposed to extreme and exceptional temperatures. In a country without widespread air-conditioning, schools, trains and sporting events remain impacted, while some 20 drowning deaths have been reported since the weekend. FRANCE 24's Caroline Baum tells us more from the Eiffel Tower in Paris.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: UK facing 'pivotal moment' as it prepares for 7th PM since Brexit vote
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer won his job after leading his party to a massive victory in 2024. Less than two years later, he resigned as his party rebelled in the wake of widespread losses in local elections. It coincides with the tenth anniversary of the Brexit vote, which led to the resignation of Conservative Prime Minister David Cameron. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot and Rochelle Ferguson-Bouyahi look at how the UK's relationship with the EU will weigh on Starmer's successor, and what led Starmer to resignation.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US, Iran agree on Lebanon ceasefire and lifting sanctions on Iranian oil, but much remains
item.description: Both Tehran and Washington hailed the negotiations that took place in Switzerland on June 22 to create a roadmap to close down the war in the Middle East. US Vice president JD Vance said it laid a good foundation for a deal to end the war. The parties agreed to establish mechanisms to open the strait of Hormuz but it is unclear who will control it.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: England: Lawn Mower Championships: 'Proper British fun'
item.description: Dozens of speed demons are competing at the British Lawn Mower Championships in Chichester, southern England.These mowers have been modified and have had their blades removed for optimal speed. They can reach an impressive 55mph (88kmph) at top speed. While often seen as a fun motorsport, the racing is incredibly physical.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24
